channel.title: High Noon Insights
channel.description: Product leadership insights from Trevor Spencer at High Noon Product.

item.title: Ideation in Product Creation: Permission to Pursue "Stupid" Ideas
item.description: The riskiest moment in product creation is the ideation stage, where an inner critic kills ideas by calling them stupid. Here's why to write them down anyway.

item.title: What Embedded Product Leadership Really Means (and How It Differs From Fractional)
item.description: A fractional product manager works part-time; embedded product leadership means owning strategy and execution through the product lifecycle. Here's what each model really means and how to tell which one your product deserves.

item.title: MetaMind: Why a Product Consultancy
item.description: In a world where anyone can build software with AI, why choose to build a product management business as a consultancy? The answer goes deeper than revenue models or product-market fit — it comes down to the collective spirit at the foundation of High Noon Product.

item.title: AI: Redemption from the Fall, or the Downfall of Humanity?
item.description: This post argues that AI, when guided by pro-human ethics and free-market accountability, mirrors three redemptive patterns from scripture: relief from compulsory labor, creation through spoken word, and the redistribution of collective intelligence.

item.title: Faith & Work
item.description: We cannot partition our lives between work and faith, especially if we want corporate culture to thrive. Here's the revelatory story behind the name and mission of High Noon Product.

item.title: Our Vision For The New World
item.description: At High Noon, we see humanity at a pivotal threshold. Here's our vision for a new era rooted in harmony, abundance, and visionary product leadership that addresses the world's greatest challenges.
